key_points: Offenhauer has biggest rooms; triple dorms workable with lofted beds
Kreischer requires multiple key fob swipes plus physical key to enter room
Centennial and Offenhauer most desired; waitlists common for top choices
key_quotes: you need to swipe your key fob like 4 times before getting up to my floor and an actual key to unlock my room door where i have mace inside
Offenhauer has the biggest rooms, you will be fine
3 person dorms are great, just gotta keep the floor clear of stuff and it feels like a hotel

key_points: Bars are walkable from campus; most students just walk, no Uber needed
Trans and queer community exists but QTSU disrupted by Ohio Senate Bill 1
Football homecoming drew 23,000+ fans; largest crowd in over a decade
key_quotes: red sky is super chill, most people are just grabbing a couple drinks, playing pool, and hanging out
As someone who is trans fem going into my 4th year at BGSU and doesn't pass all that well, I tend to avoid going downtown late at night because I've experienced a couple times...
not [fun]

key_points: Bronze meal plan is sufficient; most students have leftover swipes at semester end
Falcon Dollars cannot be used on Grubhub unless you have separate BG Bucks balance
One student reported severe gastrointestinal distress after eating at the Oaks
key_quotes: Currently attending and am currently sitting on the toilet shitting my guts out after eating at the oaks. Legit gastroenterological torture.
Dude me and my brother shared a bronze meal plan and still had swipes left over. Bronze is plenty.
Falcon dollars are a 1-1 value for dollars, but they don't work as a currency on everything

key_points: Burnout reported among education majors juggling coursework and organizations
Campus counseling exists but quality not specifically praised in student data
Research study actively recruiting students who experienced sexual assault
key_quotes: right now, I'm a little burnt out
your mental health is far more important than getting behind in school
Have you considered dropping one of your classes to take some stress off your plate?

key_points: BGSU ranked 7th nationally for student debt load
Scholarships have been quietly cut; listed awards sometimes no longer exist
Work-study pays minimum wage with no raises; over 220 applicants for single positions
key_quotes: BGSU is the number one college in Ohio for student debt, 7th nationally
BG has cut a lot of scholarships in recent years: ones listed that don't exist, and then their system will disqualify you from ones that you...
First off: recognize you will be paid minimum wage with no chance of a raise. Also, overtime was cut last semester

key_points: World Language Education program being phased out with little transparency
Music Education and Early Childhood Ed programs have strong alumni community networks
Learning Commons offers free tutoring; professors generally accessible during office hours
key_quotes: While other programs are being protected through waivers, WLED is being quietly cut
I was a music performance major in 2020-2024. You will find/make friends from ensembles, classes, and more.
Show up to office hours. No one goes to these things and the professor has time set aside for extra assistance.

key_points: Parking crisis ongoing since at least 2006 with no real solution implemented
Program cuts happening quietly without clear communication to students or prospective students
r/BGSU subreddit moderation so inactive students couldn't post for months
key_quotes: Alumni turned townie just popping in to support your fight against parking services. Shit has been garbage since 2006 and definitely before then too.
We've been saying they need to just make at least part of lot 12 a parking garage since I was there in 2015. This is bonkers
Holy shit, one of the mods woke up and unlocked the sub! This is the first post they've let through in months!

key_points: Campus is extremely flat and walkable; 10-minute gaps between buildings are manageable
Town is small; Walmart, Meijer, and Aldi are main grocery options for off-campus needs
Starship delivery robots operate on campus as a visible tech novelty
key_quotes: It's flat! very flat. I loved my years at BG
I was driving by Bowling Green and I got so surprised by seeing that robot by the sidewalk
Uber is pointless, bars are under a mile away from campus! Save your money

key_points: Geology program praised for undergrad research access due to no PhD program competing
Music Ed alumni report jobs exist but pay poorly and require geographic flexibility
CS/Software Engineering reputation unclear for out-of-state job placement
key_quotes: Great program. Not too big and no Ph.D. program gives undergrads the chance to dive deep in research.
The work is there if you're willing to relocate and not be too picky
I don't know if I'd be able to get a job OOS after that, which would be my goal

key_points: BGSU ranked 7th nationally for student debt; serious affordability red flag
Chipotle and FedEx both offer $10,500/year tuition reimbursement for employees taking classes
Flight program requires roughly $90k in loans to complete all certifications
key_quotes: BGSU is the number one college in Ohio for student debt, 7th nationally
you also forgot about the $90k student loan you're going to need to take out
Chipotle + FedEx have tuition reimbursement programs that total $10,500/year for classes passed with a C or higher

key_points: Strong school spirit around football and marching band; homecoming is a genuine event
Small college town feel; social scene is low-key bars and campus organizations
Students describe it as comfortable and friendly but not exciting or prestigious
key_quotes: BGSU Is always in my heart as an Alumnus. I will always say that my education was solid.
I absolutely love this school, and all the professors. The organizations I'm in are wonderful
In terms of sports/drinking/parties how fun is it? — not

key_points: **Debt Load:** Ranked 1st in Ohio and 7th nationally for student debt burden
**Program Cuts:** World Language Ed quietly eliminated; scholarships listed but no longer awarded
**Parking:** Crisis unsolved since 2006; students parking on unsafe muddy hillsides in winter
key_quotes: BGSU is the number one college in Ohio for student debt, 7th nationally
BG has cut a lot of scholarships in recent years: ones listed that don't exist
I graduated in 2024 and parking was an issue all four years. Good luck getting them to care unfortunately

key_points: Falcon Marching Band is 450+ members; instant social network for music students
Free store monthly at Howard's via BG DSA; real community resource for broke students
Rec center has full showers; commuters can work out between classes and clean up
key_quotes: If you are joining the Falcon Marching Band, you will automatically have over 450 friends. I met my wife in the band
Check out the BG DSA! They have a monthly free store every third Thursday at Howards
Yes the changing rooms have showers! In my gym bag I carry all of my essentials
